title: Fix application error in live/production deployment

requirements.summary: Diagnose and fix the application error that occurs when the app is accessed in the live/production environment by ensuring proper error boundaries, loading states, and actor initialization handling throughout the frontend
acceptanceCriteria: The live/production app loads without any error screen or blank page for anonymous users
The live/production app loads without any error screen or blank page for authenticated users
Internet Identity login flow completes successfully in production
Academic module, Coding module, Data Management, and User Management tabs all render correctly in production
No console errors related to actor initialization, canister calls, or missing state on first load

file_operations.description: Wrap the main app content with a React ErrorBoundary component to catch and display user-friendly error messages instead of blank screens. Add a fallback UI that shows a clear error message with retry functionality. Ensure the error boundary catches actor initialization failures, canister call errors, and any runtime exceptions. Add defensive checks for actor readiness before rendering module components. Display a loading spinner while the actor is initializing (actorFetching is true). Ensure the profile setup modal only shows when actor is ready and profile fetch is complete to prevent flashing or premature rendering.

Add error boundary wrapping around the Academic module tab content. Implement graceful error handling for failed academic data fetches with retry buttons. Show a loading state while academic entries are being fetched. Add null/undefined checks for all data before rendering charts or tables to prevent runtime errors when backend returns unexpected data shapes.

Add error boundary wrapping around the Coding module tab content. Implement graceful error handling for failed coding challenge fetches with retry buttons. Show a loading state while challenges are being loaded. Handle cases where the Pyodide runtime fails to load by displaying a clear error message to the user.

Add error boundary wrapping around Data Management. Add defensive checks before calling export/import functions to ensure the actor is ready. Show a loading state during data export/import operations. Handle authorization errors gracefully with user-friendly messages instead of letting them propagate as uncaught exceptions.

Add error boundary wrapping around User Management. Add defensive checks before calling role assignment functions to ensure the actor is ready. Show a loading state during role operations. Handle authorization errors and invalid principal errors gracefully with clear user feedback.

Review all React Query hooks to ensure they properly handle cases where the actor is null or undefined. Add enabled flags to prevent queries from running before the actor is ready. Set appropriate retry policies (retry: false or retry: 1) to avoid infinite retry loops on persistent errors. Add proper error handling with typed catch blocks. Ensure all hooks return isLoading states that account for both actor initialization and query execution. Add staleTime and cacheTime configurations to prevent unnecessary refetches that could trigger errors during migration periods.

Create a reusable ErrorBoundary component using React.Component class with componentDidCatch and getDerivedStateFromError lifecycle methods. The boundary should catch JavaScript errors, display a user-friendly error UI with the error message, and provide a 'Retry' button that resets the error state and attempts to re-render the children. Include props for custom fallback UI and error logging callbacks. Export both a class component ErrorBoundary and a higher-order component withErrorBoundary for wrapping functional components.

Create a reusable LoadingState component that displays a centered spinner with an optional loading message. Use Tailwind CSS for styling and ensure it matches the app's design system. Accept props for custom messages and spinner size. This component will be used throughout the app to show loading states while the backend actor initializes or data is being fetched.

Create a reusable ErrorMessage component that displays user-friendly error messages with optional retry functionality. Accept props for the error message, retry callback, and custom styling. Use Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui Alert components for consistent styling. Include different variants for different error severities (warning, error, info). This component will be used throughout the app to display backend errors, authorization failures, and network issues in a consistent way.

requirements.summary: Ensure the frontend handles cases where the backend actor is not yet ready or returns an error during initialization, displaying a user-friendly loading or error state instead of a blank/broken screen
acceptanceCriteria: A loading indicator is shown while the backend actor is initializing
A clear, user-friendly error message is shown if the backend cannot be reached
The app does not show a blank white screen under any initialization failure scenario

file_operations.description: Implement a top-level loading screen that displays while the actor is initializing (when actorFetching is true from useActor hook). Show the LoadingState component with a message like 'Connecting to backend...'. If actor initialization fails (actor is null after actorFetching becomes false), display the ErrorMessage component with a clear message like 'Failed to connect to backend. Please refresh the page or try again later.' and a retry button that triggers a query client invalidation. Ensure no child components render until the actor is successfully initialized or an error state is shown.

Add loading and error states for all data fetching operations. Show the LoadingState component while academic entries are being loaded. If fetching fails, show the ErrorMessage component with retry functionality. Add null checks before accessing data properties to prevent runtime errors when data shape is unexpected. Ensure charts only render after data is successfully loaded and validated.

Add error handling for the addAcademicEntry mutation. Display the ErrorMessage component if the submission fails with a clear message about what went wrong. Disable the submit button while the actor is not ready or while a submission is in progress. Show a loading spinner on the submit button during submission. Handle validation errors gracefully and display them inline in the form.

Add error handling for Pyodide initialization failures. Display the ErrorMessage component if Pyodide fails to load with a message like 'Failed to load Python runtime. Please refresh the page.' Add error handling for the saveCodingAttempt mutation. Show loading states during code execution and attempt saving. Handle cases where the actor is not ready by disabling submission and showing an appropriate message.

Add loading and error states for academic entries data fetching. Show the LoadingState component while data is loading. Display the ErrorMessage component with retry if fetching fails. Add null/undefined checks before computing subject statistics to prevent runtime errors. Ensure the chart only renders after successful data validation.

Add loading and error states for academic entries fetching. Show the LoadingState component while loading. Display the ErrorMessage component if fetching fails. Add defensive checks before converting grades to prevent errors when data is malformed or missing expected fields. Ensure all numeric conversions handle null/undefined gracefully.

Add loading and error states for grade aggregates fetching. Show the LoadingState component while data loads. Display the ErrorMessage component with retry if fetching fails. Add null checks before computing combined percentages to handle missing term data gracefully. Ensure the component renders an empty state message when no data is available instead of breaking.

Add loading and error states for academic entries fetching. Show the LoadingState component while loading. Display the ErrorMessage component if fetching fails. Add defensive checks before calculating achievement tiers to prevent errors when entries are missing required fields. Ensure certificate images (frontend/public/assets/generated/certificate-platinum.dim_400x250.png, certificate-gold.dim_400x250.png, certificate-silver.dim_400x250.png, certificate-bronze.dim_400x250.png) load correctly and handle image load errors with fallback UI.

Add loading and error states for coding attempts fetching. Show the LoadingState component while attempts are loading. Display the ErrorMessage component with retry if fetching fails. Add defensive checks before rendering attempt details to handle missing or malformed data gracefully. Show an empty state message when no attempts exist instead of rendering nothing.
